name: Background Worker Runtime

userStory.iWant: to run my application's logic and view generation in a background thread
userStory.soThat: the main thread remains 100% responsive to user input and TTY writes, even during heavy processing.

acceptanceCriteria: A WorkerThreadAdapter exists for Node.js (and Wasm/Web)
The TEA loop (init/update/view) runs entirely within the Worker
The Main thread handles only raw I/O and final Surface blitting
Message serialization/deserialization is handled transparently by the proxy
The UI maintains a steady 60fps even if the Worker is under load (render lagging, but not input lagging)
backwardCompatibility: Applications can switch to Worker mode by swapping the runtime.run() call for runtime.runWorker(). Application-level code (App definition) remains identical.

outScope: Auto-parallelization of component rendering (vertical scaling within the worker)

testPlan.goldenPath: Run a TUI that performs a 500ms synchronous block in its update function. Assert that the main thread still processes a 'Quit' keypress instantly.
edgeCases: Messages containing non-serializable data (functions, class instances)
Worker thread crashing (should notify main thread and exit gracefully)
Rapid message flooding from main to worker
testPlan.fuzzStress: Send 1 million messages through the worker bridge and measure the serialization overhead latency.

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(S) where S is size of serialized message
